source_intake_record.raw_question_text: e. The electromagnet is composed of layers of spiral wound copper pipe; the pipe has inner diameter $d_{i}=0.40 \mathrm{~cm}$ and outer diameter $d_{o}=0.50 \mathrm{~cm}$. The copper pipe is wound into this flat spiral that has an inner diameter $D_{i}=20 \mathrm{~cm}$ and outer diameter $D_{o}=50 \mathrm{~cm}$. Assuming the pipe almost touches in the spiral winding, determine the length $L$ in one spiral.
extraction_notes: 题干中出现了 `<image_1>` 占位符，且有 `image_1` 图像对象字段，但未提供可直接提取的图片路径/URL。
答案字段原值为 `C`，已按选项映射提取为 `33`；解答中写作 `33 \mathrm{~m}`，但选项文本本身不含单位。

image_dependency: `requires_image=false` explicitly indicates the problem is not intended to depend on the image.
`text_dominant=true` and the full geometry/dimensions are provided in the question text.
Although `image_manifest` contains 1 PNG, no indispensable visual attribute is referenced in the wording.
multi_step: The solver must combine several quantities: pipe inner/outer diameter and spiral inner/outer diameter.
The phrase 'pipe almost touches in the spiral winding' implies an inferred spacing/turn-count step before total length can be estimated.
This is a geometry/setup problem rather than direct lookup; it likely needs at least two reasoning steps (determine turns/radii, then aggregate spiral length).
verifiability: `answer_type` is numeric and the item is multiple-choice, which supports objective checking.
The question provides concrete dimensions and assumptions, so a deterministic calculation path exists.
`registry_passed=true`, `question_present=true`, and `answer_present=true` indicate the sample is structurally complete.
Conservative reduction: `answer_text` is just `33` while the question asks for a length, so unit/form interpretation may need care.
